# Template_Lakefile — Fully Annotated Lakefile Template

This file serves as a COMPREHENSIVE reference for a typical `lakefile.lean`.
It documents every option, explains the tradeoffs, and shows CI patterns.

**Usage**: Copy and adapt. Keep only what you need.
This file mirrors the CURRENT project lakefile structure at:
  `<your-lean-dir>/lakefile.lean`
with extensive annotation of all available options.

For the ACTUAL lakefile, see `lakefile.lean` in the project root.

## References
* [Lake documentation](https://leanprover-community.github.io/lean4/doc/lake.html)
* [Mathlib lake setup](https://leanprover-community.github.io/mathlib4_docs/lake.html)
* [Lake DSL reference](https://lean-lang.org/lean4/doc/lake.html)

## Tags
template, lake, lakefile, build, CI, package, lean_lib, require

```lean
-- NOTE: This is a TEMPLATE reference file. The actual lakefile.lean
-- uses `import Lake; open Lake DSL` at the top.
-- See `<your-lean-dir>/lakefile.lean` for the live version.
```

## §1  The Actual Lakefile (Annotated)

```lean
import Lake
open Lake DSL

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- PACKAGE DECLARATION
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

package MyProject where
  -- REQUIRED: Package name (must match directory name for lake conventions)
  name := "MyProject"

  -- OPTIONAL: Semantic version (Major.Minor.Patch)
  -- version := Version 0 1 0

  -- ── leanOptions: compile-time Lean options ──────────────────────────
  -- These apply to ALL lean_lib targets in this package.
  leanOptions := #[
    -- REQUIRED: Disable autoImplicit globally.
    -- Without this, typos create silent universe-polymorphic variables.
    ⟨`autoImplicit, false⟩,

    -- OPTIONAL (stricter): Also disables relaxedAutoImplicit.
    -- ⟨`relaxedAutoImplicit, false⟩,

    -- OPTIONAL: Suppress unused variable warnings globally.
    -- ⟨`linter.unusedVariables, false⟩,

    -- OPTIONAL: Suppress tactic state output in #check.
    -- ⟨`pp.funBinderTypes, true⟩,

    -- PITFALL: Do NOT set maxHeartbeats here — it affects ALL modules.
    -- Set it locally with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in theorem ...`
  ]

  -- OPTIONAL: moreLinkArgs for C FFI or native extensions
  -- moreLinkArgs := #["-L/usr/local/lib", "-lmylibrary"]

  -- OPTIONAL: precompileModules true
  -- Makes Lake compile modules to .olean files eagerly.
  -- Useful for large libraries where many files are imported.
  -- Can slow initial build but speeds up downstream compilation.
  -- precompileModules := true

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- DEPENDENCIES
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- ── mathlib ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- REQUIRED: Mathlib provides all analysis, algebra, tactics.
-- ALWAYS pin to a specific tag (e.g., v4.28.0) for reproducibility.
-- PITFALL: Using `main` or `master` branch breaks reproducibility.
require mathlib from git
  "https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4.git" @ "v4.28.0"

-- ── cslib ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- For example: cslib for Qq (type-safe quotation in custom tactics).
-- Pin to a specific commit hash for reproducibility.
require cslib from git
  "https://github.com/leanprover/cslib" @ "11b70e5"

-- ── OPTIONAL: Adding other dependencies ─────────────────────────────────
-- require MyPackage from git
--   "https://github.com/org/MyPackage" @ "v1.2.3"
--
-- For local development dependencies:
-- require MyLocalLib from "../my-local-lib"
--
-- PITFALL: Local paths (../) break in CI if the path doesn't exist.
-- Use `require ... from git ...` for CI-compatible dependencies.

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- LEAN LIBRARY TARGETS
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- ── Single-target configuration (CURRENT project) ─────────────────────────
-- Simple: one lean_lib covers the entire Lean module tree.
-- Drawback: ANY change invalidates the entire cache for importers.

@[default_target]
lean_lib MyProject where
  srcDir := "."
  -- globs := #[.andSubmodules `MyProject]  -- implied by srcDir + library name
  -- roots := #[`MyProject]                 -- explicit root module

-- ── RECOMMENDED: Multi-target configuration (for large projects) ─────────
-- Split into separate lean_lib targets = finer-grained build invalidation.
-- Changing MyProject.Foundations only rebuilds targets that import it,
-- NOT the entire your project library.

-- TEMPLATE: Fine-grained multi-target lakefile (uncomment and adapt):
-- @[default_target]
-- lean_lib MyProjectFoundation where
--   globs := #[
--     .submodule `MyProject.Tactics,
--     .submodule `MyProject.Foundations,
--     .submodule `MyProject.Classifiers,
--     .submodule `MyProject.Profiling,
--   ]
--
-- lean_lib MyProjectLayer1 where
--   globs := #[
--     .submodule `MyProject.Catastrophe,
--     .submodule `MyProject.Predicates,
--   ]
--
-- lean_lib MyProjectLayer2 where
--   globs := #[
--     .submodule `MyProject.Stochastic,      -- if split: andSubmodules
--     .submodule `MyProject.Safety,
--     .submodule `MyProject.Pipeline,
--   ]
--
-- lean_lib MyProjectMain where
--   globs := #[.andSubmodules `MyProject]  -- catches anything not in above targets

-- PERFORMANCE NOTE: With multi-target setup, `lake build` builds only
-- the default_target. Use `lake build MyProjectLayer1` to build just that layer.
-- CI: `lake build` (default) or `lake build -j $(nproc)` (parallel).

-- ── Release builds (v4.28+) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
-- buildType := .release  -- compile with -O2 optimization
-- Use in CI for performance benchmarking, not for proof checking.

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- EXECUTABLES (if you have any lean programs in the project)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- TEMPLATE: If you have a Lean executable (e.g., a proof checker driver):
-- lean_exe myTool where
--   root := `MyTool.Main
--   -- supportInterpreter := true  -- allows `#eval` in the exe module

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- EXTERNAL LIBRARY TARGETS (for C FFI)
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- TEMPLATE: If your project includes C libraries:
-- extern_lib myCLib where
--   srcDir := "c-src"
--   -- TEMPLATE: Lake will call make in srcDir
```

## §2  lean-toolchain File

```
leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0
```

TEMPLATE: Create `lean-toolchain` (no extension) in the project root.
This pins the exact Lean version.
Lake reads this file and refuses to build with a different Lean version.

PITFALL: `lean-toolchain` and `lake-manifest.json` must be in sync.
After `lake update`, commit BOTH files together.

## §3  Build Commands

```bash
# Standard build:
lake build

# Parallel build (use all CPU cores):
lake build -j $(nproc)

# Build a specific module:
lake build MyProject.Predicates

# Fetch Mathlib pre-built .olean cache (REQUIRED before first build):
lake exe cache get

# Update all dependencies to latest pinned versions:
lake update

# Check for unused imports (v4.28):
lake shake

# Clean build artifacts:
lake clean

# Build and run tests (if you have a Test executable):
lake exe myTestRunner
```

## §4  lake-manifest.json Notes

The `lake-manifest.json` is AUTO-GENERATED by `lake update`.
NEVER edit it by hand.
Always commit it alongside `lakefile.lean` for reproducibility.

After running `lake update`, verify:
1. `lake-manifest.json` has the expected commit hashes
2. `lean-toolchain` version matches the manifest
3. `lake build` succeeds from clean state

## §5  CI YAML Snippet (GitHub Actions)

```yaml
name: Lean Proof Check

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install elan (Lean version manager)
        run: |
          curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/leanprover/elan/master/elan-init.sh \
            -sSf | sh -s -- -y
          echo "$HOME/.elan/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      - name: Get Mathlib cache
        # Fetches pre-built .olean files to avoid full Mathlib compilation.
        # This step can save 30-60 minutes of build time.
        run: lake exe cache get

      - name: Build MyProject
        run: lake build -j $(nproc)
        timeout-minutes: 60  # Adjust based on your module count

      # OPTIONAL: Run proof statistics
      # - name: Count theorems
      #   run: python census.sh  # or whatever your census script is
```

## §6  Troubleshooting Common Lake Issues

### "unknown package 'cslib'"
Run `lake update` to fetch the dependency manifest.

### "version mismatch" errors
Ensure `lean-toolchain` matches the Lean version in `lake-manifest.json`.

### Slow initial build
Run `lake exe cache get` BEFORE `lake build` to fetch Mathlib's .olean cache.
Without the cache, Mathlib takes 1-3 hours to compile from source.

### "import cycle detected"
Your DAG layer ordering has a cycle. Use `#import_path SomeDecl` (v4.28, takes
declaration names not module names) to trace the cycle. Refactor to remove the cycle.

### Build cache invalidation for large modules
Files > 3000 lines cause all their importers to recompile on every edit.
Split them into .Core / .Lemmas / .Main (see Template_Application.lean §7).

## §7  leanOptions Reference

```lean
-- Linting options:
⟨`linter.all, true⟩                    -- enable all linters
⟨`linter.unusedVariables, false⟩       -- suppress unused variable warnings
⟨`linter.style.longLine, false⟩        -- suppress 100-char line warnings
⟨`linter.docPrime, false⟩              -- allow `foo'` without docstring

-- Proof options:
⟨`autoImplicit, false⟩                 -- REQUIRED: no silent implicits
⟨`relaxedAutoImplicit, false⟩          -- stricter: no relaxed implicits
⟨`maxHeartbeats, 400000⟩               -- AVOID: set per-theorem instead

-- Display options (for interactive use, not CI):
⟨`pp.all, false⟩                       -- don't show full universe annotations
⟨`pp.funBinderTypes, true⟩             -- show types in fun binders
```

## §8  Checklist for lakefile.lean Updates

Before committing a lakefile change:
- [ ] `lake build` succeeds from clean state
- [ ] `lake exe cache get` works (correct Mathlib version)
- [ ] `lake-manifest.json` committed alongside lakefile.lean
- [ ] `lean-toolchain` version matches manifest
- [ ] All `require` pins use specific tags/commits (not `main`)
- [ ] `leanOptions` includes `autoImplicit := false`
- [ ] Multi-target lean_lib structure (for projects > 5 modules)
- [ ] CI YAML updated if new targets were added

## §9  Memory and Parallelism

-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- MEMORY AND PARALLELISM
-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- ── Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Set these in your shell profile, CI config, or wrapper script.
-- They are NOT set in lakefile.lean — they are process-level.
--
-- LEAN_THREAD_STACK_SIZE=33554432     -- 32MB stack (default ~8MB)
-- LEAN_MAX_MEMORY=4294967296          -- 4GB max heap (default: unlimited)
-- LEAN_INITIAL_HEAP_SIZE=536870912    -- 512MB initial heap
-- LEAN_GC_HEAP_LIMIT_GROWTH=2.0       -- GC growth factor
-- LEAN_GC_STATS=1                     -- print GC stats (debugging)
--
-- ── Parallel Build ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- lake build -j$(nproc)   -- use all CPU cores
-- lake build -j8          -- or specify count
-- LAKE_JOBS=8             -- alternative: environment variable
--
-- Parallelism is at MODULE granularity:
-- - Modules in the same DAG layer build in parallel
-- - Splitting large modules increases parallelism
-- - Multiple lean_lib targets enable independent rebuilds

## §10  CI Script Template

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Example CI build script
set -euo pipefail

export LEAN_THREAD_STACK_SIZE=33554432     # 32MB
export LEAN_MAX_MEMORY=4294967296          # 4GB

cd <your-lean-dir>
lake exe cache get                          # download Mathlib oleans
lake build -j$(nproc) -q --log-level=info   # parallel, quiet
lake shake                                  # check for unused imports (v4.28)

# Verification checks
grep -rn "sorry" <lean-dir>/ --include="*.lean" | grep -v "^--" | grep -v "doc" && exit 1
grep -rn "^axiom" <lean-dir>/ --include="*.lean" && exit 1
echo "✅ Build + verification passed"
```
